name: cargo
description: Rust package manager and build system

subcommands.name: new
subcommands.description: Create a new Cargo package

args.name: <path>
args.description: Directory path for the new package

options.name: --name
options.description: Set the package name (defaults to directory name)

options.name: --vcs
options.description: Version control system to initialize (git, hg, pijul, fossil, none)

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Create a new Cargo package in an existing directory

options.name: --vcs
options.description: Version control system (git, hg, pijul, fossil, none)

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Compile the current package and all dependencies

options.name: --features
options.description: Space or comma-separated list of features to activate

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

options.name: --test
options.description: Build only the specified test target

options.name: --all-targets
options.description: Build all targets (lib, bins, tests, benches, examples)

options.name: --workspace
options.description: Build all members of the workspace

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Use verbose output (-vv for very verbose)

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Build and execute the main binary of the local package

args.name: <args>
args.description: Arguments to pass to the binary (after --)

options.name: --release
options.description: Run in release mode with optimizations

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Execute all unit tests, integration tests, and doc tests

options.name: --lib
options.description: Test only the library unit tests

options.name: --test
options.description: Test only the specified integration test

options.name: --tests
options.description: Test all targets with test = true

options.name: --no-run
options.description: Compile but do not run tests

options.name: --no-fail-fast
options.description: Run all tests regardless of failure

options.name: --workspace
options.description: Test all members of the workspace

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: bench
subcommands.description: Execute all benchmarks of a local package

options.name: --test
options.description: Benchmark only the specified test target

options.name: --no-run
options.description: Compile but do not run benchmarks

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Analyze the current package and report errors without producing output binary

options.name: --test
options.description: Check only the specified test target

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Remove artifacts that cargo has generated in the past

options.name: --profile
options.description: Remove artifacts for the specified profile

options.name: --target
options.description: Remove artifacts for the target triple

subcommands.name: doc
subcommands.description: Build this package's and its dependencies' documentation

options.name: --open
options.description: Open the docs in a browser after building

options.name: --no-deps
options.description: Do not build documentation for dependencies

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: publish
subcommands.description: Upload a package to the registry (crates.io by default)

options.name: --token
options.description: API token to use for authentication

options.name: --no-verify
options.description: Do not verify the contents by building them

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Build and install a Rust binary crate

args.name: <crate>
args.description: Crate to install (omit for current directory)

options.name: --branch
options.description: Branch to use when installing from git

options.name: --tag
options.description: Tag to use when installing from git

options.name: --rev
options.description: Specific commit to use when installing from git

options.name: --path
options.description: Install from a local filesystem path

options.name: --force
options.description: Force overwriting existing crates or binaries

options.name: --locked
options.description: Require Cargo.lock is up to date

options.name: --frozen
options.description: Require Cargo.lock and cache are up to date

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

options.name: --list
options.description: List all installed packages and their versions

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Remove a Rust binary installed with cargo install

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Search packages in crates.io

options.name: --limit
options.description: Maximum number of results to display

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update dependencies as recorded in the local lock file

options.name: --aggressive
options.description: Force updating all dependencies of the specified packages

options.name: --precise
options.description: Update a single dependency to exactly the specified version

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Show what would be updated without actually updating

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Add dependencies to a Cargo.toml manifest file

options.name: --features
options.description: Space or comma-separated features to activate

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Show what would be added without actually modifying Cargo.toml

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Remove dependencies from a Cargo.toml manifest file

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Show what would be removed without modifying Cargo.toml

subcommands.name: tree
subcommands.description: Display a tree visualization of the dependency graph

options.name: --depth
options.description: Maximum depth of the dependency tree

options.name: --invert
options.description: Invert the tree direction (show what depends on a package)

options.name: --duplicates
options.description: Show packages with multiple versions only

options.name: --edges
options.description: Edge types to display (normal, dev, build, all, features, no-dev, no-build, no-normal)

options.name: --format
options.description: Format string for each package line

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: metadata
subcommands.description: Output resolved dependencies of a package in machine-readable JSON format

options.name: --no-deps
options.description: Output only workspace members without fetching dependencies

options.name: --filter-platform
options.description: Only include dependencies for the given target

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: fix
subcommands.description: Automatically fix lint warnings reported by rustc and clippy

options.name: --edition
options.description: Fix code to be compatible with the next edition

options.name: --edition-idioms
options.description: Fix code to use the current edition idioms

options.name: --broken-code
options.description: Fix code even if it will not compile after fixes

options.name: --test
options.description: Fix only the specified test target

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: clippy
subcommands.description: Run Clippy lints on the current package

options.name: --allow-dirty
options.description: Allow fixing with uncommitted changes (used with --fix)

options.name: --allow-staged
options.description: Allow fixing with staged changes (used with --fix)

options.name: --test
options.description: Lint only the specified test target

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: fmt
subcommands.description: Format all Rust files of the current crate using rustfmt

options.name: --check
options.description: Check formatting without modifying files (exit with error if unformatted)

options.name: --all
options.description: Format all packages in the workspace

subcommands.name: audit
subcommands.description: Audit Cargo.lock for crates with security vulnerabilities (requires cargo-audit)

options.name: --deny
options.description: Exit with an error if any vulnerabilities are found matching category

options.name: --no-fetch
options.description: Do not fetch the advisory database

subcommands.name: outdated
subcommands.description: Display when newer versions of dependencies are available (requires cargo-outdated)

options.name: --depth
options.description: How deep in the dependency chain to search

options.name: --aggressive
options.description: Ignore the SemVer compatibility and check for the latest version

subcommands.name: vendor
subcommands.description: Vendor all dependencies locally into a directory

options.name: --no-delete
options.description: Do not delete older crates in the vendor directory

subcommands.name: generate-lockfile
subcommands.description: Generate the lockfile without building anything

subcommands.name: package
subcommands.description: Assemble the local package into a distributable tarball

options.name: --list
options.description: Print files included in the package

options.name: --no-verify
options.description: Do not verify the contents by building them

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Save an API token to the local credential store

args.name: <token>
args.description: API token (reads from stdin if not provided)

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Remove an API token from the local credential store

subcommands.name: owner
subcommands.description: Manage the owners of a crate on the registry

options.name: --add
options.description: Name of a user or team to invite as an owner

options.name: --remove
options.description: Name of a user or team to remove as an owner

subcommands.name: yank
subcommands.description: Remove a pushed crate from the index (does not delete the crate)

options.name: --undo
options.description: Undo a yank, putting the version back into the index

subcommands.name: fetch
subcommands.description: Fetch dependencies of a package from the network

options.name: --target
options.description: Fetch dependencies for the target triple

subcommands.name: locate-project
subcommands.description: Print the path to the Cargo.toml manifest file

subcommands.name: verify-project
subcommands.description: Check the correctness of a Cargo.toml manifest

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Show version information for cargo

subcommands.name: rustc
subcommands.description: Compile the current package and pass extra flags to the compiler

options.name: --test
options.description: Build only the specified test target

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: rustdoc
subcommands.description: Build a package's documentation, passing extra flags to rustdoc

options.name: --test
options.description: Document only the specified test target

options.name: --no-default-features
options.description: Do not activate the default feature

subcommands.name: report
subcommands.description: Generate and display various reports

globalOptions.name: --verbose
globalOptions.description: Use verbose output (-vv for very verbose)

globalOptions.name: --frozen
globalOptions.description: Require Cargo.lock and cache are up to date

globalOptions.name: --locked
globalOptions.description: Require Cargo.lock is up to date

globalOptions.name: --explain
globalOptions.description: Run rustc --explain CODE to show detailed error explanation

examples: cargo new myapp && cd myapp && cargo run
cargo test && cargo clippy && cargo fmt --check
cargo add serde -F derive && cargo add serde_json
cargo install cargo-watch && cargo watch -x run

detectors.name: cargo_toml
detectors.description: Read the Cargo.toml manifest for package metadata, dependencies, and features

detectors.name: workspace_members
detectors.description: List all workspace member packages and their paths

detectors.name: installed_binaries
detectors.description: List all globally installed cargo binary crates

detectors.name: targets
detectors.description: List all build targets (bins, libs, tests, examples, benches) in the current package

detectors.name: dependencies
detectors.description: List direct dependencies and their versions using cargo tree

detectors.name: rust_version
detectors.description: Detect the active Rust compiler version

detectors.name: toolchain
detectors.description: Detect the active Rust toolchain (stable, nightly, etc.)

detectors.name: available_targets
detectors.description: List installed compilation targets (e.g., wasm32, musl)

detectors.name: test_targets
detectors.description: Discover compiled test executables for the current package

detectors.name: clippy_warnings
detectors.description: Get a summary of current Clippy lint warnings

detectors.name: outdated_deps
detectors.description: List dependencies with newer versions available (requires cargo-outdated)
